model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed-step evaluation (256 optimizer steps), increasing batch size exposes the model to more unique training examples within the same step budget, which often improves short-horizon validation loss. A moderate LR increase complements the larger batch to maintain optimization progress per step.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100","new":"    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current cosine schedule is effectively flat for this short run because T_max is extremely large. Enabling scheduling and matching T_max to fixed-step budget should provide useful decay within 256 steps and improve end-of-budget validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous step degraded performance, so this reverts scheduler behavior and tests a modest capacity increase in convolutional and classifier width to improve representational power under the same 256-step budget without a major complexity jump.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After the successful capacity increase, a moderately higher learning rate can improve optimization progress within the fixed 256-step budget; this is a minimal, high-leverage follow-up change.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With improved LR and capacity, switching from Adam to AdamW can improve generalization under short training by decoupling weight decay from adaptive moments while keeping the rest of the tuned setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW regressed versus the best visible result, so reverting to Adam restores the stronger optimizer baseline before further tuning.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step training, increasing batch size raises the number of training examples seen within the same 256 updates, which can improve validation loss if optimization remains stable.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling batch size to 256 successfully, increasing learning rate proportionally can improve progress per update in the fixed 256-step regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 updates, slightly reducing regularization can lower underfitting and improve end-of-budget validation loss while keeping the rest of the tuned setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance has improved consistently with larger effective update scale, so a small additional LR increase is a targeted next step to extract more progress within the fixed 256 updates.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last learning-rate increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to the previously better rate restores the strongest known configuration before testing new directions.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With optimization now tuned for the fixed-step regime, adding one more residual block is a minimal depth increase that can improve feature quality and reduce validation loss without changing the training loop.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After increasing model depth, a small further reduction in weight decay can ease underfitting in this short 256-step regime while preserving the current successful optimization setup.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Current setup is stable and improving; a small LR increase is a low-complexity probe for a slightly better optimization rate without the larger regression risk seen at 2.25e-3.","secondary_modes":["caching","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 2.1e-3 probe caused a large regression, so reverting to 2e-3 restores the strongest known learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"For a short fixed-step run, lowering Adam beta2 can make second-moment estimates adapt faster and improve early optimization efficiency without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta2 regressed validation loss, so restoring the previous Adam betas returns to the stronger known optimizer setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With convolutional depth already increased and optimizer tuned, a modestly wider classifier head may better exploit learned features and improve validation loss with minimal code complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The wider classifier head caused a clear regression, so reverting to the previous head width restores the stronger known architecture.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 320","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed optimizer-step budget and stable optimization settings, a moderate batch-size increase can expose the model to more unique samples across 256 steps and may further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}
